# Cortex

> Cortex is a local-first, evidence-guided kernel for software-engineering agents. It preserves a
> durable case file, explicit hypotheses and disproof paths, bounded change ownership, provenance,
> and revision-bound verification across long tool-using tasks.

Use the documentation below as the canonical product guide. The source code and contributor
guidance live at https://github.com/abdul-hamid-achik/cortex.

## Start here

- [Overview](https://cortexai.tools/): Product purpose, core guarantees, and the reasoning loop.
- [Quick start](https://cortexai.tools/quick-start): Install Cortex and complete the golden path.
- [Full tutorial](https://cortexai.tools/tutorial): Retry safety, evidence, planning, leases, verification, and preservation.
- [Guide for agents](https://cortexai.tools/agents): Agent-specific operating rules and recovery behavior.

## Interfaces

- [MCP server](https://cortexai.tools/mcp): Compact agent profile, complete operator profile, tool schemas, and examples.
- [CLI reference](https://cortexai.tools/cli): Human commands and machine-readable JSON equivalents.
- [Studio](https://cortexai.tools/studio): Cross-repository operator board and canonical session projection.

## Kernel model

- [Concepts](https://cortexai.tools/concepts): Phase machine, evidence discipline, disproof, ownership, and verification semantics.
- [Case file](https://cortexai.tools/case-file): Durable JSON/JSONL layout, coordination, and recovery.
- [Configuration](https://cortexai.tools/configuration): XDG paths, budgets, redaction, recall, and configured verifiers.
- [Adapters](https://cortexai.tools/adapters): Specialist-tool routing and safe degradation.
- [FAQ](https://cortexai.tools/faq): Common failure and recovery questions.

## Golden path

`open → investigate → plan → begin-change → verify → remember`

Prefer `cortex_open_task` over a fresh start. Follow the first machine-readable `actions` item returned
by each successful result. Treat `ok: false` or an MCP error result as a rejected operation. Never call
a change complete unless the canonical assessment is `verified`, or the user explicitly accepts a
partial, failed, or unverified preservation outcome. When success rules are known, register immutable
`acceptanceCriteria` and verify the same IDs and exact statements; acknowledgments cannot bypass them.
Use `cortex_status.claimProofs` for bounded proof identity. Cortex's default 17-tool MCP profile and
90 KiB complete-proof handoffs are compatible with local-agent directly or through MCPHub.
